tool.name: PHASTEST
tool.description: PHAge Search Tool with Enhanced Sequence Translation - rapid identification and annotation of prophage sequences
tool.command:
echo "Start tool"
&& echo "id:" && id
## Set up input file #if $input_type.input_selector == 'fasta' or $input_type.input_selector == 'contig' && ln -s '$input_type.sequence_file' /phastest_inputs/input_sequence.fna #end if
## Run PHASTEST && echo "Running PHASTEST..." && phastest -i $input_type.input_selector #if $input_type.input_selector == 'genbank' -a '$input_type.accession' #else -s input_sequence.fna #end if -m $annotation_mode #if $phage_only --phage-only #end if --yes --silent
&& mkdir -p output && mv \$PHASTEST_HOME/JOBS/*/* output/
#if $input_type.input_selector == 'contig' && mv output/*_positions.txt output/positions.txt #end if

param.name: input_selector
param.label: Input format
param.help: Select the type of input data for PHASTEST analysis

param.name: sequence_file
param.label: FASTA sequence file
param.help: Bacterial genome or plasmid sequence in FASTA format. Minimum sequence length is 1500bp.

param.name: sequence_file
param.label: Contig sequence file
param.help: Whole genome shotgun contigs in FASTA format

param.name: accession
param.label: GenBank accession number
param.help: Accession number for the genome (e.g., NC_000907.1, KF030445.1). For WGS sequences, provide the master record accession number. Requires internet connection to download from NCBI.

param.name: annotation_mode
param.label: Annotation mode
param.help: 'lite' uses Prophage Database and Swissprot (faster). 'deep' uses Prophage Database and PHAST-BSD Bacterial Database (more comprehensive but slower).

param.name: phage_only
param.label: Annotate phage regions only
param.help: If enabled, only predicted phage regions will be annotated. By default, whole genomes are scanned and annotated.

tool.help:
.. class:: infomark
**What it does**
PHASTEST (PHAge Search Tool with Enhanced Sequence Translation) provides rapid identification and annotation of prophage sequences within bacterial genomes and plasmids. It is compatible with:
- GenBank nucleotide records (complete sequences or whole genome shotgun sequences) - Nucleotide sequence files in FASTA format
PHASTEST identifies prophage regions, annotates genes, and provides detailed information about the prophage structure and potential function.
**Inputs**
There are three input options:
1. **FASTA sequence**: Upload a bacterial genome or plasmid sequence in FASTA format (minimum 1500bp)
2. **Contig sequence**: Upload whole genome shotgun contigs in FASTA format
3. **GenBank accession**: Provide an accession number (e.g., NC_000907.1, KF030445.1, LZPG00000000.1) to automatically download and analyze the sequence from NCBI. Note: This requires internet connection and depends on NCBI server availability.
**Parameters**
- **Annotation mode**:
- *Lite*: Uses Prophage Database and Swissprot. Faster processing, suitable for most analyses. - *Deep*: Uses Prophage Database and PHAST-BSD Bacterial Database. More comprehensive but takes significantly longer to complete.
- **Annotate phage regions only**: When enabled, only the predicted phage regions will be annotated. By default, whole genomes are scanned and annotated.
**Outputs**
- **Summary**: High-level summary of identified prophage regions - **Detailed results**: Comprehensive annotation of prophage sequences and genes - **Annotated GenBank**: GenBank format file with annotated prophage regions
**Notes**
- For FASTA/contig inputs, minimum sequence length is 1500bp - Deep annotation mode may take significantly longer to complete - GenBank input requires internet connection and depends on NCBI server status
.. class:: infomark
**References**
For more information about PHASTEST, visit the official website at https://phastest.ca/
